query_normalized: analyzing the trade‑off between prompt size and code generation quality computer science
rationale: This paper addresses the user's question by investigating the impact of context management (a proxy for prompt size and information density) on code generation quality and accuracy in multi-agent systems, satisfying criteria (a) for measuring the same mechanism and (b) for evaluating the dependent variable (code quality) in the user's domain. Although it proposes a specific workflow rather than a pure trade-off curve, it provides essential empirical evidence on how context engineering strategies
